briefs.title: BC Tightens EV Range Rules and Credit System for Auto Suppliers Starting 2026
briefs.summary: BC updated its zero-emission vehicle rules, cutting the minimum range for battery electric vehicles from 241 km to 150 km and raising plug-in hybrid range requirements to 80 km by model year 2029. It also changes how ZEV credits (government points awarded for selling clean vehicles) are issued starting October 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Review your ZEV vehicle lineup and credit strategy to ensure compliance with new range thresholds by model year 2026.

key_dates.label: BC ZEV new range thresholds take effect — model year 2026 vehicles
BC ZEV credit issuance rules change under updated regulation
BC ZEV plug-in hybrid 80 km minimum range requirement takes effect — model year 2029
